rulings.Case Title: AJLOUN ENTERPRISES, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. GLEN LEIS et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 16, 2014, Line 10: DEFENDANT GLEN LEIS' Motion To Compel: DENIED. Defendant's motion to compel responses to request for production and special interrogatories is not supported by a separate statement. CRC 3.1345. Defendant's motion to compel responses to Form Interrogatories -Limited is not supported by a proper separate statement. CRC 3.1345(c)(3). Form Interrogatories - General are not designed to be used as a part of unlimited number of form interrogatories in unlawful detainer cases. Even though defendant is not precluded from using such interrogatories in lieu of/or as a supplement to specially prepared interrogatories, there has been insufficient meet and confer on the specific questions prior to the filing of this motion. Verification has been provided prior to the filing of this motion and the issue is moot. Sanctions granted in the amount of $550. (501/LKS)

rulings.Case Title: XIAOQI WU VS. ROBIN SHAPIRO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Extablish Admissions From Defendant, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 16, 2014, Line 6: PLAINTIFF XIAOQI WU's Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant: GRANTED, subject to opposition. Defendant shall provide responses to Form Interrogatories -UD, Special Interrogatories, Request for Production within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Admissions are deemed admitted. Sanctions granted in the amount of $945. (501/LKS)

rulings.Case Title: ZAFIRI LIMITED PARTNERSHIP VS. WILLIAM SEELEY et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 16, 2014, Line 7: DEFENDANT WILLIAM SEELEY's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT: OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges the amount of rent due under the rental agreement and the amount of daily holdover damages. Moving party cites no authority for the proposition that those amounts have to match, as they do not have to. See . Haig v. Hogan (1947) 82 Cal.App.2nd 876. Complaint sufficiently alleges basis for recovery of attorney's fees in paragraph 13. No authority is cited for the proposition that lease must be attached to complaint when attorney's fees are sought in the action based on non-payment of rent. See CCP 1166(d) To the extent Defendant is challenging the prayer for attorney's fees, such prayer had to be addressed by the motion to strike. (501/LKS)
